title: MUniverse Grison et al 2025
description: High-density surface electromyography (HDsEMG) recordings from the tibialis anterior muscle of one healthy subject during submaximal isometric ankle dorsiflexions at force levels ranging from 10 to 70 percent maximum voluntary contraction. The dataset comprises 128-channel recordings from two electrode arrays and includes labeled motor unit spike trains derived from concurrent invasive EMG recordings. This dataset supports investigation of motor unit recruitment and control strategies during graded voluntary contractions.
methods_description: HDsEMG signals were recorded from the right tibialis anterior using two 64-electrode arrays (4 mm interelectrode distance, 13×5 configuration) during isometric ankle dorsiflexions at 10-70% MVC. Contractions consisted of trapezoidal force profiles with linear ramps at 5%/second and plateaus of 20s (≤30% MVC), 15s (35-40% MVC), or 10s (50-70% MVC), separated by 120s rest periods. Force was recorded simultaneously using a load cell connected to a commercial dynamometer. Motor unit spike trains were labeled from concurrent invasive EMG recordings.
